description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly gray surface, interspersed with patches of tan and off-white coloration. The stone features a variety of natural indentations, shallow pits, and subtle ridges, giving it a weathered and organic appearance. Its surface is matte and rough, with visible mottling and streaks that suggest mineral deposits or exposure to the elements. The coloration is uneven, with some areas appearing darker and more saturated, while others are lighter and more faded. The stone is solid and dense, likely composed of a type of sedimentary or metamorphic rock. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban landscapes such as parks or sidewalks.
description: An irregular, gray and tan mottled stone with a rough, weathered surface and natural indentations. Its solid, dense form and varied coloration suggest exposure to the elements.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rounded, bulbous end of the stone with prominent gray and tan streaks and a few shallow surface pits.
description_view_1: The stone appears elongated and flatter from this angle, with deeper indentations and a more pronounced mix of gray and tan patches.
description_view_2: This perspective shows a smoother, less detailed side with subtle color transitions and fewer visible surface features.
description_view_3: The broadest side is visible here, displaying extensive mottling and a large, irregular tan patch amidst the gray surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and streaks of gray, tan, and off-white, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte, with visible signs of natural weathering such as shallow pits and faded coloration.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces, sometimes partially surrounded by grass or soil.
